column1: # Shape bridge — defines the TARGET design pattern and the transformation rules.
# Each row maps a source class (from_id) to a target class (to_id) and optionally
# declares a new relation (relation_id → target_id) that will be added between
# target instances.
# The '-' placeholder in from_id/to_id is forward-filled from the previous row
# allowing multiple new relations to be declared for the same class mapping.
# is_root: marks which source class should be used as sh:targetClass in the SHACL
#   shape. Only one row should be marked true. If none is marked
#   automatically via closeness centrality.
# mapping_justification uses SSSOM-style vocabulary:
#   semapv:ManualMappingCuration — human expert reviewed and approved this mapping
#   semapv:StructuralMatching   — mapping follows structural equivalence of graph patterns
# Columns:
#   from_id             — CURIE of the source class
#   to_id               — CURIE of the target class
#   relation_id         — CURIE of the new relation added in the target pattern (or '-')
#   target_id           — CURIE of the target of the new relation (or '-')
#   is_root             — true/false; marks the SHACL shape anchor class
#   mapping_justification — SSSOM mapping justification CURIE
#   comment             — human-readable explanation of why this mapping is valid
from_id
ex:Process
column7: A Process as an investigative activity maps to an Experiment. The hasExperimentSetup relation is the structural equivalent of hasPart in the source pattern.

column1: ex:Process
column7: An Experiment also directly references its Sample; this reflects the flattening of the Input→ProcessStep→Process chain.

column1: ex:ProcessStep
column7: A ProcessStep as a configuration step maps to ExperimentSetup. The performedWith relation connects it to Parameters (equivalent to InputSettings).

column1: ex:Input
column7: An Input as a material specimen maps to Sample. The hasConcentrations relation connects it to Parameters, reflecting the InputSettings role.

column1: ex:InputSettings
column7: InputSettings maps to Parameters. No additional outgoing relation is declared here; Parameters is a terminal node in the target pattern.
